extraction.one_line_summary: GARCÍA CORTINA Ivan won a chaotic sprint where BENNETT Sam and EWAN Caleb were blocked by HOFSTETTER Hugo with 200m remaining.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE Julian attacked inside 10km after TERPSTRA Niki crash, forcing splits before sprint teams could reorganize.
SAGAN Peter bridged to ALAPHILIPPE, suggesting Bora prioritized GC defense over sprint leadout for ACKERMANN Pascal.
MATTHEWS Michael led out BOL Cees but entire train stalled, revealing hesitation when favorites are eliminated mid-sprint.
Israel Startup Nation let domestique GARCÍA CORTINA Iván finish after protecting all day, exploiting chaos when sprinters waited.

rider_signals.GARCÍA CORTINA Iván: positive: workhorses who protect early can capitalize when leadout trains collapse in chaotic finishes
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: crashed/blocked in sprint, bloodied hand, Israel showing vulnerability when positioning is compromised
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: HOFSTETTER Hugo locked him out from behind with 200m to go, completely blocked despite good position
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: bridged to ALAPHILIPPE attack but hesitated in sprint when favorites eliminated, prioritizing GC over stage
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: tactically astute attack inside 10km post-crash forced GC teams to react when sprint teams disorganized
rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: negative: suffered bike change inside 10km then caught in TERPSTRA crash, doubly eliminated
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: led out BOL but train stalled when sprinters waited for eliminated favorites to clear
rider_signals.DEVRIENDT Tom: neutral: solo breakaway 190km gaining 9:15, demonstrates futility of lone rider in headwind regardless duration
